title: Set 1.2 - Key Distribution, Diffie-Hellman, SHA/HMAC & Trust Models
subject: PKI
topic: Key Distribution, Diffie-Hellman, SHA/HMAC & Trust Models

questions.question: What is the primary challenge in key distribution for symmetric key cryptography?
questions.description: Correct answer: Securely sharing keys between parties.

questions.question: Which key exchange protocol is commonly used to establish a secure communication channel over an insecure network?
questions.description: Correct answer: Diffie-Hellman.

questions.question: In public-key cryptography, how is the public key shared with others?
questions.description: Correct answer: By publishing it openly.

questions.question: What is a digital certificate used for in the context of key management?
questions.description: Correct answer: To prove identity.

questions.question: What does the term "key escrow" refer to in key management?
options: Storing keys in a secure location
Backup of keys with a trusted third party
questions.description: Correct answer: Backup of keys with a trusted third party.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a nonce in key generation?
questions.description: Correct answer: Providing randomness in key generation.

questions.question: What is the primary function of a cipher in the context of cryptography?
questions.description: Correct answer: To encrypt and decrypt messages.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the Diffie-Hellman key exchange?
questions.description: Correct answer: Key generation.

questions.question: Which security aspect does Diffie-Hellman key exchange address?
questions.description: Correct answer: Key exchange.

questions.question: In the Diffie-Hellman key exchange, what is exchanged between the parties?
questions.description: Correct answer: Public keys.

questions.question: What is the vulnerability of Diffie-Hellman key exchange to man-in-the-middle attacks?
questions.description: Correct answer: Lack of authentication.

questions.question: Which mathematical problem forms the basis of the security of Diffie-Hellman?
questions.description: Correct answer: Discrete logarithm.

questions.question: What is the key advantage of using Diffie-Hellman key exchange over traditional key exchange methods?
questions.description: Correct answer: Perfect forward secrecy.

questions.question: In a Diffie-Hellman exchange, what is kept secret by each party?
questions.description: Correct answer: Private key.

questions.question: Which Diffie-Hellman variant provides resistance against quantum attacks?
questions.description: Correct answer: DH with elliptic curves.

questions.question: What is the main limitation of Diffie-Hellman key exchange?
questions.description: Correct answer: Vulnerability to quantum attacks.

questions.question: In Diffie-Hellman, what role does the generator play?
questions.description: Correct answer: Facilitates key exchange.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the SHA algorithm?
questions.description: Correct answer: Hashing.

questions.question: Which version of SHA is commonly used for cryptographic applications?
questions.description: Correct answer: SHA-256.

questions.question: What is the key characteristic of a secure hash function?
questions.description: Correct answer: Collision resistance.

questions.question: Which of the following is a weakness of SHA-1?
questions.description: Correct answer: Vulnerability to birthday attacks.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the HMAC (Hash-based Message Authentication Code) function?
questions.description: Correct answer: Message authentication.

questions.question: In HMAC, what role does the secret key play?
questions.description: Correct answer: Authenticates the message.

questions.question: Which SHA variant is often used in digital signatures?
questions.description: Correct answer: SHA-256.

questions.question: What is a rainbow table attack in the context of hash functions?
questions.description: Correct answer: Precomputed table attack.

questions.question: Which property of SHA makes it suitable for checksum verification?
questions.description: Correct answer: Deterministic output.

questions.question: In SHA, what is the purpose of the initialization vector (IV)?
questions.description: Correct answer: Adds entropy to the hash.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of HMAC?
questions.description: Correct answer: Message authentication.

questions.question: What distinguishes HMAC from traditional hash functions?
questions.description: Correct answer: Use of a secret key.

questions.question: In HMAC, what role does the hash function play?
questions.description: Correct answer: Computes the hash.

questions.question: Why is the use of a secret key important in HMAC?
questions.description: Correct answer: Provides authentication.

questions.question: What vulnerability does HMAC address in comparison to traditional hash functions?
questions.description: Correct answer: Length extension attacks.

questions.question: In HMAC, how is the secret key shared between the communicating parties?
options: It is transmitted in the clear
It is generated by the hash function
It is negotiated and agreed upon
It is embedded in the public key
questions.description: Correct answer: It is negotiated and agreed upon.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the inner and outer padding in HMAC?
questions.description: Correct answer: Ensures a consistent block size.

questions.question: Which property of HMAC ensures that changing a single bit in the input will produce a substantially different output?
questions.description: Correct answer: Avalanche effect.

questions.question: In HMAC, what is the consequence of using a weak hash function?
questions.description: Correct answer: Weakened authentication.

questions.question: How does HMAC provide security against brute-force attacks?
options: It uses a long initialization vector
It requires a strong secret key
It uses a reversible hashing algorithm
questions.description: Correct answer: It requires a strong secret key.

questions.question: What is a brute-force attack against encryption?
questions.description: Correct answer: Attempting all possible keys.

questions.question: In a man-in-the-middle attack, what does the attacker intercept?
questions.description: Correct answer: Ciphertext.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of a chosen plaintext attack?
questions.description: Correct answer: Recovery of the encryption key.

questions.question: What is the difference between a passive and an active attack?
options: Active attacks modify data, while passive attacks only observe
Passive attacks use brute force, while active attacks use social engineering
Active attacks are faster than passive attacks
Passive attacks are more dangerous than active attacks
questions.description: Correct answer: Active attacks modify data, while passive attacks only observe.

questions.question: What vulnerability does a chosen ciphertext attack exploit?
options: Manipulation of the key exchange process
Inability to handle large amounts of data
questions.description: Correct answer: Lack of authentication.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a birthday attack against encryption?
questions.description: Correct answer: Finding collisions in hash functions.

questions.question: Which type of attack targets the random number generation in cryptographic protocols?
questions.description: Correct answer: Side-channel attack.

questions.question: What is the objective of a chosen-plaintext attack?
questions.description: Correct answer: Interception of plaintext.

questions.question: How does a rainbow table attack work against encrypted passwords?
options: Precomputes hash values for possible passwords
questions.description: Correct answer: Precomputes hash values for possible passwords.

questions.question: What is the primary defense against a brute-force attack on encryption?
options: Use of long and complex passwords
questions.description: Correct answer: Use of strong encryption algorithms.

questions.question: What is a digital signature?
options: A scanned image of a handwritten signature
A unique identifier for a digital document
A cryptographic technique to verify the authenticity of a digital message
A file attachment in an email
questions.description: Correct answer: A cryptographic technique to verify the authenticity of a digital message.

questions.question: Which key is used to create a digital signature?
questions.description: Correct answer: Private key.

questions.question: What does a digital signature provide?
questions.description: Correct answer: All of the above.

questions.question: In a digital signature, what is being signed?
questions.description: Correct answer: A hash of the document.

questions.question: What role does hashing play in digital signatures?
options: Verifies the integrity of the message
questions.description: Correct answer: Verifies the integrity of the message.

questions.question: Which algorithm is commonly used for digital signatures?
questions.description: Correct answer: SHA-256.

questions.question: How does a digital signature differ from a handwritten signature?
options: Digital signatures can be easily copied
Handwritten signatures require a pen and paper
questions.description: Correct answer: Digital signatures are more secure.

questions.question: What is the purpose of timestamping in digital signatures?
options: To indicate the time the document was created
To verify the expiration date of the signature
questions.description: Correct answer: To indicate the time the document was created.

questions.question: Can a digital signature be revoked?
options: No, once created, it cannot be revoked
Yes, through a Certificate Revocation List (CRL)
questions.description: Correct answer: Yes, through a Certificate Revocation List (CRL).

questions.question: What is a digital certificate?
options: A file format for storing digital images
A digital identity that includes a public key and information about the key owner
questions.description: Correct answer: A digital identity that includes a public key and information about the key owner.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a digital certificate?
options: To verify the authenticity of a message or user
questions.description: Correct answer: To verify the authenticity of a message or user.

questions.question: Which organization issues digital certificates?
options: Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers (ICANN)
questions.description: Correct answer: Certification Authority (CA).

questions.question: What information does a digital certificate typically contain?
options: Public key, owner's name, expiration date, and the digital signature of the CA
questions.description: Correct answer: Public key, owner's name, expiration date, and the digital signature of the CA.

questions.question: How is a digital certificate linked to a public key?
options: By digitally signing the public key with the CA's private key
By embedding the public key in the certificate
questions.description: Correct answer: By digitally signing the public key with the CA's private key.

questions.question: What is the role of the Registration Authority (RA) in the issuance of digital certificates?
options: Verifying the identity of the certificate owner
questions.description: Correct answer: Verifying the identity of the certificate owner.

questions.question: Can a digital certificate expire?
options: Yes, but only if manually revoked
Yes, they have a validity period
No, it expires only when the private key is compromised
questions.description: Correct answer: Yes, they have a validity period.

questions.question: How is the integrity of a digital certificate maintained?
options: By hashing the certificate and signing it with the CA's private key
By including a timestamp in the certificate
questions.description: Correct answer: By hashing the certificate and signing it with the CA's private key.

questions.question: What is the Public Key Infrastructure (PKI)?
options: A system for managing public keys and digital certificates
questions.description: Correct answer: A system for managing public keys and digital certificates.

questions.question: How can a user verify the authenticity of a digital certificate?
options: By checking the owner's email address
By verifying the certificate's expiration date
By validating the digital signature with the CA's public key
By contacting the Internet Service Provider (ISP)
questions.description: Correct answer: By validating the digital signature with the CA's public key.

questions.question: What is a Trust Model?
options: A model of trust in interpersonal relationships
A framework for establishing and managing trust in a digital environment
questions.description: Correct answer: A framework for establishing and managing trust in a digital environment.

questions.question: In a Trust Model, what is the role of a Trust Anchor?
options: The root of trust in the model
The person who manages the trust relationships
questions.description: Correct answer: The root of trust in the model.

questions.question: What are the common components of a Trust Model?
options: Trust Anchors, Certificate Authorities (CAs), and Registration Authorities (RAs)
questions.description: Correct answer: Trust Anchors, Certificate Authorities (CAs), and Registration Authorities (RAs).

questions.question: How does a Trust Model establish trust in a digital environment?
options: By creating a hierarchical structure of trust relationships
questions.description: Correct answer: By creating a hierarchical structure of trust relationships.

questions.question: What is the significance of the Chain of Trust in a Trust Model?
options: It represents a physical chain worn by trusted individuals
It is a sequence of trust relationships from a Trust Anchor to an end entity
It is a type of digital certificate
It ensures secure communication between devices
questions.description: Correct answer: It is a sequence of trust relationships from a Trust Anchor to an end entity.

questions.question: In a Web of Trust model, who establishes trust relationships?
questions.description: Correct answer: End users themselves.

questions.question: How is trust propagated in a Hierarchical Trust Model?
options: Through a centralized authority establishing trust at different levels
questions.description: Correct answer: Through a centralized authority establishing trust at different levels.

questions.question: What is the advantage of a Distributed Trust Model?
options: It is more resilient to single points of failure
It eliminates the need for digital certificates
It speeds up the trust establishment process
questions.description: Correct answer: It is more resilient to single points of failure.

questions.question: In a Circle of Trust model, what is the key principle?
options: Trust is established in a linear fashion
Trust is established in a circular manner between entities
Trust is established through biometric authentication
Trust is established through physical tokens
questions.description: Correct answer: Trust is established in a circular manner between entities.

questions.question: How does a Federated Trust Model work?
options: By delegating trust responsibilities to multiple independent organizations
By relying on a single Certificate Authority (CA)
questions.description: Correct answer: By delegating trust responsibilities to multiple independent organizations.

questions.question: What is the role of a Certification Authority (CA)?
options: To issue digital certificates and verify the identity of the certificate owner
To authenticate users based on biometric data
questions.description: Correct answer: To issue digital certificates and verify the identity of the certificate owner.

questions.question: How does a CA verify the identity of a certificate applicant?
questions.description: Correct answer: By checking government-issued identification.

questions.question: Can there be multiple CAs in a public key infrastructure?
options: No, there is only one CA in any infrastructure
Yes, but they must share a single private key
Yes, multiple CAs can exist independently
No, CAs are specific to individual users
questions.description: Correct answer: Yes, multiple CAs can exist independently.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a Certificate Signing Request (CSR)?
options: To request a new digital certificate from the CA
To revoke an existing digital certificate
To request a private key from the CA
To update the digital certificate information
questions.description: Correct answer: To request a new digital certificate from the CA.

questions.question: How is the CA's public key distributed?
options: By including it in every digital certificate
By broadcasting it on a public server
By sharing it with other CAs
By encrypting it with the CA's private key
questions.description: Correct answer: By including it in every digital certificate.

questions.question: What is a Root Certificate?
options: The certificate issued by the end entity
The top-level certificate in a CA hierarchy
The public key of a CA
questions.description: Correct answer: A self-signed certificate.

questions.question: How often does a CA issue a Certificate Revocation List (CRL)?
options: Only when a digital certificate expires
Whenever there is a change in the CA's public key
Only when a certificate is revoked before expiration
questions.description: Correct answer: Only when a certificate is revoked before expiration.
